title: I stopped losing on "valid" setups when I started asking one question before every trade
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Not "will this work?"

But "is this actually structured?"

There's a difference.

A setup can look clean, feel right, have confluence — and still be garbage.

Because you're seeing what you want to see.

I've blown three accounts that way. Not from bad analysis. From confirmation bias dressed up as technical analysis.

What changed for me : — I stopped entering until I could define my invalidation in one sentence — If I couldn't explain why price would NOT go my way, I didn't enter — I started treating every setup like I was trying to prove it wrong, not right

Win rate didn't change much. RR did. Dramatically.

Last week : XAU/USD 2.15 RR. EUR/USD 3.15 RR. BTC 2.51 RR.

Not because the market was easy. Because I stopped taking bad setups.

What's the one thing that most improved your consistency?

comments.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
comments.body:
For context — I've been building a tool that forces this second-check process before you enter.

Upload a chart screenshot, the AI analyzes the structure and tells you if the setup holds up. Kills the bias loop faster than journaling alone.

Free to try : [https://herculmarket.com](https://herculmarket.com)
